Data: hey! May I know what you mean by the delay in the thesis? there are instances when you reach the rd year, in the st sem you start formulating the thesis and then in the nd sem it's experimentation and writing the manuscript, but if you enroll for the third year, and then you still have back subjects in other subs or don't pass the pre req for thesis, sometimes the department will advise you not to do the thesis that year, but sometimes there will be a request esp if you go in person and bring a parent, but there are also instances that you were able to conduct the experiment always in the thesis and made the manuscript but your experiment failed, not a failed experiment because that was the result produced by the experiment, but he failed because you had lapses during the experiment that could have been prevented ex. wrong storage conditions, wrong drying, etc. You failed the thesis sub and will have to do it again next year, hope this helps

Data: congrats on getting accepted op!! to answer some of your questions: - the usc physics society is a co-curricular organization that you're automatically a member of once you enroll as an appphys major and under that org is the astronomy society which is open to all carolinians rather than being limited to only the physics students. - the campus is relatively close to it park and ayala central bloc, and there are a number of cafes and workspaces nearby: - there are also some dorms nearby and on-campus although you'll need to check them out personally to see if they're within your budget. - the shuttle only travels within the campus and is really helpful for going between your classes if your classes are in different buildings; having the time to go in and out of campus is tbh based on your class schedule and whether you have ample time in between each class. hopefully this helps!
English_Translation: congrats on getting accepted op!! to answer some of your questions: - the usc physics society is a co-curricular organization that you're automatically a member of once you enroll as an appphys major and under that org is the astronomy society which is open to all carolinians rather than being limited to only the physics students. - the campus is relatively close to it park and ayala central bloc, and there are a number of cafes and workspaces nearby: - ​​there are also some dorms nearby and on-campus although you'll need to check them out personally to see if they're within your budget. - the shuttle only travels within the campus and is really helpful for going between your classes if your classes are in different buildings; having the time to go in and out of campus is tbh based on your class schedule and whether you have ample time in between each class. hopefully this helps!

Data: hi, im planning to enroll for chem. Eng. in usc, it would be my first time enrolling on my own, and its far from home, im not familiar with the campus. any thoughts and tips regarding the program in usc and upon enrolling?
English_Translation: hi, im planning to enroll for chem. eng. in usc, it would be my first time enrolling on my own, and its far from home, im not familiar with the campus. any thoughts and tips regarding the program in usc and upon enrolling?

Data: hey! bs chemical engineering graduate here. this program is among the most demanding in usc. in terms of training, it's pretty great though. its faculty had quite a reputation of being unfriendly like the teachers we'd be happy to have because they aren't as strict as the others are still scary for those outside bs che. but the chairman right now is "rehabilitating" this image, and is quite successful in that regard. a good thing about the training in usc is that there are tons of opportunities upon graduating. some of the non-core subjects we have are focused on adjacent-but-not-quite-che skills like management, a bit of law and ethics, taste testing and others. quite a number of us are in the academy like me, some are doing well in industries despite the sort of abysmal initial pay, and others have successfully pivoted to other fields quite easily because our training still applies. just a fair warning that many students don't graduate on time there though. about half of the time it's not a failing grade; there's just this emphasis on research, and teachers might ask you to extend for a bit more to produce a good output. luckily enough, our opportunities when graduating are still good and usc che is quite famous among companies with che's, so you can deffo bank on that. having said that, if you're someone who'd just get over college as soon as possible, this might not be for you. but if you're the type who'd go slow and steady to get the best experience out of something, bs che might work great. PS: a common mistake of incoming students is that they expect that there will be a lot of chemistry in this program. granted we still have more chemistry compared to other engineering programs, but usc's training has more emphasis on math, bio and then physics. gotta say though that the physics bs che has is quite different and more fun imho than the physics in shs.

Data: up up up, also want to know about higher year students' thoughts. I just commented a little bit because I shared the cost in the past and posted a similar question, but as an incoming nd year pharma student, I can't tell you from another school because I know you, so it's purely usc. curriculum is good unlike the old one that didn't have summer, soa cramped because many majors, the pacing is objectively breathable, maybe in pharma as a course it's challenging by default. lab equipments and facilities are complete, the students are not cramped, then the teacher to student ratio is good. anyways away from the technical stuff. subjectively. overall nice man usc pharma, the learning experience is competent and holistic. but to answer your question... a small background, I'm aiming for school or course anything as long as it's health allied, and I didn't expect to be in usc because of our financial situation but because of dost, I was placed here.. and this links to second question. Many of my kahunas have moved even some of my batchmates, the system doesn't feel very good, I just struggle a lot with this program, especially balancing dorm life, personal life and leisure. I really don't know how I got decent grades because I'm always in survival mode in subjects and everyday I have doubts, maybe because I have an affinity like actual engineering, but my passion and really lies on healthcare, even though I'm going to be a patient. but if no financial concerns and heavy personal struggles, or even perhaps I have few concerns and my socioeconomic status allows me to focus on acads.. i would still choose usc, sincerely. because compared to what I heard from my classmates who already know pharma from other cebu schools, the environment here is nice, laboratories, the faculty, orgs. but yah there goes the student retention policy which is a big concern for us too, that's why the decrease in students is severe.

Data: hi! just a question because I've been reading here on reddit and people say that the entrance exam is so easy and it's just for formality. is this true po? does usc just accept all applicants?
English_Translation: hi! just a question because i've been reading here on reddit and people say that the entrance exam is so easy and it's just for formality. is this true po? does usc just accept all applicants?

Data: hi op, it really depends on the person. yes, the coverage of the entrance exam is mostly common knowledge and only touches the basics of topics on the shs strands. the downside of the exam though is that you're only given a short amount of time to answer, so you can't really say it's nothing to worry about. moreover, different departments actually require students in certain performances such as average in mathematics for stem related courses. if a student does not meet these requirements, they are however referred to a different course. so, usc does accept all applicants but it does not guarantee that students are offered the course of their interest.
English_Translation: hi op, it really depends on the person. yes, the coverage of the entrance exam is mostly common knowledge and only touches the basics of topics on the shs strands. the downside of the exam though is that you're only given a short amount of time to answer, so you can't really say it's nothing to worry about. moreover, different departments actually require students in certain performances such as average in mathematics for stem related courses. if a student does not meet these requirements, they are however referred to a different course. so, usc does accept all applicants but it does not guarantee that students are offered the course of their interest.

Data: hey there, op! just wanted to give you a heads up about the cet. i'm an incoming freshman too, but i took it already and let me tell you, it's not that bad i passed, woohoo!. they say it's more of a formality to check if you're keeping up academically, not some genius filter. from my experience, the test wasn't super hard. it depends on your major though, i'm in stem so there was a lot of that stuff. but weirdly, there were also questions on stuff like sociology and literature who knew, right?. basically, it's math, english, some light coremajor subjects, and a quick iq test. you don't need to be a master of anything, just brush up on the general stuff you learned in senior high school really though, just study a little bit. most importantly, relax! eat your lunch and get a good night's sleep beforehand, there's a snack break in the middle of the test, and hey, i didn't study at all due to certain difficulties, still totally not recommended, but hey, it worked for me!. the tests are mostly minutes each, so keep an eye on the clock and don't be afraid to skip a question and come back later. mine took about hours and minutes in total. i can't speak for the school itself yet i didn't get to attend yet, but from what i've researched and heard from students, usc seems pretty awesome, living up to that top ranking in the philippines. good luck with your test, and hope to see you on campus soon!
English_Translation: hey there, op! just wanted to give you a heads up about the cet. i'm an incoming freshman too, but i took it already and let me tell you, it's not that bad i passed, woohoo!. they say it's more of a formality to check if you're keeping up academically, not some genius filter. from my experience, the test wasn't super hard. it depends on your major though, i'm in stem so there was a lot of that stuff. but weirdly, there were also questions on stuff like sociology and literature who knew, right?. basically, it's math, english, some light coremajor subjects, and a quick iq test. you don't need to be a master of anything, just brush up on the general stuff you learned in senior high school really though, just study a little bit. most importantly, relax! eat your lunch and get a good night's sleep beforehand, there's a snack break in the middle of the test, and hey, i didn't study at all due to certain difficulties, still totally not recommended, but hey, it worked for me!. the tests are mostly minutes each, so keep an eye on the clock and don't be afraid to skip a question and come back later. mine took about hours and minutes in total. i can't speak for the school itself yet i didn't get to attend yet, but from what i've researched and heard from students, usc seems pretty awesome, living up to that top ranking in the philippines. good luck with your test, and hope to see you on campus soon!

Data: just to give you a really general explanation of this the retention policy entails that it must be in all professional courses meaning those major subjects courses must all grades that you get will not fall below.. there are possibilities to this not saying that everything must be above. because shet just first is under monitoring, which occurs when you obtain grades that fall below., or under probation which occurs when you have grades below.. a student can also fall under probation as well when he gets a failing grade. but there is a limit to failing because if more than the total units fail then it will be counted for transfer. basically, in the following semesters your grades should be maintained above. to regain good standing. otherwise, if you are under monitoring then you will be under probation in the following semester. however, if you are already under probation then case to case basis you will only be allowed to enroll in a limited number of units or you will be advised to transfer out. this isnt meant to intimidate future carolinians from applying, just for awareness. honestly, the program is just that intense. The retention policy was only recently implemented, so we are a little confused about this. to survive the program, you need to develop effective and efficient study habits that are good for you. pharma at USC is also worth it especially if you are looking for laboratory experience because Matik is one of the best in the Philippines.

Data: hi po! the north campus is okay, but there are times where the comfort rooms are unavailable, which makes it very inconvenient i'll also add that the canteen, at the usual lunch hours, are very full, so it's better for you to bring your own lunch and water since there's only one canteen in the north campus. as for the environment and the people there, it depends mostly on your blockmates and the people you surround yourself with, but in my experience, it was pretty nice. as for the subjects, i actually answered that before, you can check it in this comment for the most part, the teachers are pretty good. of course, everyone has their favorites, and there's a few teachers that are very fun but are still able to teach their subjects well. some teachers are debatable, but that's why it's really important for you to make connections with your peers.

Data: hi! I'm an incoming grade stem student at USC and I agree with what mango_bumblebee said. I love the teachers and the way they teach, especially in the math and science departments. in usc, we call honors as cad awardees. you have to get at least in every subject to be a cad awardee. and back then in the first sem, it seems that there are only cad awardees per block out of students in a block haha but there are many in the second sem because they have adjusted. watch out for chemistry, pre-calculus, and general mathematics for first sem and still chemistry and basic calculus in second sem maybe with statistics too but i had fun w it. There was one time my bright classmate in math because he was not included in cad since he was in filipino and there was another subj, gen math, so he didn't miss it. but be grateful since the line of is very common. lots will be thankful na with line of but still aim high and don't make that your mindset if you want to excel. my only tip is take down notes, make sure your teachers know your name right away, have a positive mindset, and be friendly especially with other blocks because I have many friends from other blocks and we share answers or get info about presentations or projects. if you don't mind me asking, who are your teachers? I might probably know some of them.

Data: hi, long time carolinian here. ill answer no. for you originally, north and south used to be separate campuses both hosting basic edu. and shs. however, in s. y. -, a merge occurred for basic edu. to make space for shs students which lead to all basic edu. of usc to be transferred to south campus. as you know currently, the north campus hosts the abm and stem students while the south campus hosts the humss and ad students. regarding the merging specifically, the original intention was to turn north campus as the senior high campus, where all the academic strands would be in one campus. unfortunately due to various issues in administrative procedures, it was delayed, leaving humss and ad students to remain in south. it is unlikely for the merging to occur within this upcoming school year s. y. -. the merging will likely occur next school year s. y. - or within years after that.

Data: Hello! timeline is. years minimum due to internship and thesis. that's if you are not employed and focused and psych is your undergrad degree. you can finish the coursework in. years at least units per sem, one sem of comprehensive exams, one sem of internship and one year of thesis. tuition for units is around k probably more as they implemented an increase this sem. schedule of classes is weekdays, pm onwards, rarely on saturday. usc grad school is not in a trimester calendar so its will really take time. realistically here's my experience: I started in early and completed coursework in. I look for a leave of absence of one sem during the lockdowns. I did my internship for a year as I was employed -. Data: For accurate and detailed assistance regarding scholarships, I strongly recommend emailing the scholarship office directly at scholarship. they are the best resource to guide you through the application process and answer any specific questions you may have. if you achieved excellent grades in senior high school, be sure to highlight your academic accomplishments. submit your grade report and any evidence of extracurricular activities to strengthen your application. you'll also need to complete the scholarship application form, provide a certification proving you were among the top three students in your strand, and write a compelling -word essay. in merit scholarships, additional documents like your parents' income tax return and a copy of your family's electric bill might be required, as financial need is often considered. while strong cae results are beneficial, they might not be the deciding factor in scholarship awards, so your results might not matter that much. just focus on presenting a well-rounded application that showcases your achievements, potential, and passion for your chosen field of study. I wish you the best of luck in your scholarship pursuits!!

Data: hey! bs chemical engineering graduate here. this program is among the most demanding in usc. in terms of training, it's pretty great though. its faculty had quite a reputation of being unfriendly like the teachers we'd be happy to have because they aren't as strict as the others are still scary for those outside bs che. but the chairman right now is "rehabilitating" this image, and is quite successful in that regard. a good thing about the training in usc is that there are tons of opportunities upon graduating. some of the non-core subjects we have are focused on adjacent-but-not-quite-che skills like management, a bit of law, taste testing and others. quite a number of us are in the academy like me, some are doing well in industries despite the sort of abysmal initial pay, and others have successfully pivoted to other fields quite easily because our training still applies. just a fair warning that many students don't graduate on time there though. about half of the time it's not a failing grade; there's just this emphasis on research, and teachers might ask you to extend for a bit more to produce a good output. luckily enough, our opportunities when graduating are still good and usc che is quite famous among companies with che's, so you can deffo bank on that. having said that, if you're someone who'd just get over college as soon as possible, this might not be for you. but if you're the type who'd go slow and steady to get the best experience out of something, bs che might work great. ps: a common mistake of incoming students is that there will be a lot of chemistry in this program. granted we still have more chemistry compared to other engineering programs, but usc's training has more emphasis on math, bio and then physics. gotta say though that the physics bs che has is quite different and more fun imho than the physics in shs. pps: I've read from your history that you're also looking into med, op. I know a couple of people who went the che-to-med school route, and they're doing fine so far. even if i said we don't really focus on chemistry in usc che, a lot of biochemistry is integrated into class, and i heard it helps a lot.
English_Translation: hey! bs chemical engineering graduate here. this program is among the most demanding in usc. in terms of training, it's pretty great though. its faculty had quite a reputation of being unfriendly like the teachers we'd be happy to have because they aren't as strict as the others are still scary for those outside bs che. but the chairman right now is "rehabilitating" this image, and is quite successful in that regard. a good thing about the training in usc is that there are tons of opportunities upon graduating. some of the non-core subjects we have are focused on adjacent-but-not-quite-che skills like management, a bit of law, taste testing and others. quite a number of us are in the academy like me, some are doing well in industries despite the sort of abysmal initial pay, and others have successfully pivoted to other fields quite easily because our training still applies. just a fair warning that many students don't graduate on time there though. about half of the time it's not a failing grade; there's just this emphasis on research, and teachers might ask you to extend for a bit more to produce a good output. luckily enough, our opportunities when graduating are still good and usc che is quite famous among companies with che's, so you can deffo bank on that. having said that, if you're someone who'd just get over college as soon as possible, this might not be for you. but if you're the type who'd go slow and steady to get the best experience out of something, bs che might work great. ps: a common mistake of incoming students is that there will be a lot of chemistry in this program. granted we still have more chemistry compared to other engineering programs, but usc's training has more emphasis on math, bio and then physics. gotta say though that the physics bs che has is quite different and more fun imho than the physics in shs. pps: i've read from your history that you're also looking into med, op. i know a couple of people who went the che-to-med school route, and they're doing fine so far. even if i said we don't really focus on chemistry in usc che, a lot of biochemistry is integrated into class, and i heard it helps a lot.

Data: how is the polsci program at usc? it's great if you like reading, writing, and public speaking because the staples of the program are readings, oral recitations, and research- and essay-writing. the polsci profs are absolutely great as well since they're all very considerate. they offer consultation hours where you could talk about anything from sharing academic stresses, asking for help on a topic that stumped you, etc. and do you think minoring in econ would be a good decision? I'm in the minor in econ program as well and I don't regret it. know that it doesn't start until second year though on a related note, the majors in lps or irfs also don't start until third year. if you can manage the polsci work well enough and you're hungry for more, then minoring in econ would definitely complement your polsci knowledge. a lot of the economics concepts and methods discussed in your polsci subjects will make a lot more sense. you'll also see the world and its problems through an economics perspective.
English_Translation: how is the polsci program at usc? it's great if you like reading, writing, and public speaking because the staples of the program are readings, oral recitations, and research- and essay-writing. the polsci profs are absolutely great as well since they're all very considerate. they offer consultation hours where you could talk about anything from sharing academic stresses, asking for help on a topic that stumped you, etc. and do you think minoring in econ would be a good decision? i'm in the minor in econ program as well and i don't regret it. know that it doesn't start until second year though on a related note, the majors in lps or irfs also don't start until third year. if you can manage the polsci work well enough and you're hungry for more, then minoring in econ would definitely complement your polsci knowledge. a lot of the economics concepts and methods discussed in your polsci subjects will make a lot more sense. you'll also see the world and its problems through an economics perspective.

English_Translation: that's great to know! i love reading and writing, even when i have to do it out of necessity. public speaking's a whole other deal for me, but i can always work on that. thanks for the heads up regarding when minors and majors start in usc. it's has a pretty similar timeline to my current uni's biology majors micro and med. how does the transfer process work? is it a hassle? and how is the usc bapos culture? are the people great? what about orgs?

Data: how does the transfer process work? is it a hassle? transfer process was super smooth. just follow the steps in the enrollment guide and you're good. the only big things to note are the entrance essay and the interview imho, both are just formalities to make sure you're not a totally crazy person. nothing to sweat over. it's better to apply early since they process it much faster. once you get to the end of the enrollment period, they'll be swamped so your application progresses slower. and how is the usc bapos culture? are the people great? there's definitely a lot of very vocal and extroverted people who are good at the oral recitals and group settings. there's also a few introverts who are silent assassins in written exams. everyone fits into a smaller clique of friends. generally, most are polite and outgoing. the big sin that'll earn you a lot of enemies quickly is to slack off on the group projects. what about orgs? almost everyone i know is in an org. it's practically expected of you to join more than one university-wide org or one within the slg. of course there's cpss, the polsci student org, and scire, the polsci student pub. today's carolinian is also a common place for some polsci writers to end up in. the carolinian debate society is another common polsci student pick. there's lots of advocacy organizations as well as from environmentalism, student leadership, etc.
English_Translation: how does the transfer process work? is it a hassle? transfer process was super smooth. just follow the steps in the enrollment guide and you're good. the only big things to note are the entrance essay and the interview imho, both are just formalities to make sure you're not a totally crazy person. nothing to sweat over. it's better to apply early since they process it much faster. once you get to the end of the enrollment period, they'll be swamped so your application progresses slower. and how is the usc bapos culture? are the people great? there's definitely a lot of very vocal and extroverted people who are good at the oral recitals and group settings. there's also a few introverts who are silent assassins in written exams. everyone fits into a smaller clique of friends. generally, most are polite and outgoing. the big sin that'll earn you a lot of enemies quickly is to slack off on the group projects. what about orgs? almost everyone i know is in an org. it's practically expected of you to join more than one university-wide org or one within the slg. of course there's cpss, the polsci student org, and scire, the polsci student pub. today's carolinian is also a common place for some polsci writers to end up in. the carolinian debate society is another common polsci student pick. there's lots of advocacy organizations as well as from environmentalism, student leadership, etc.

Data: Hello! in usc, all polsci students start out as polsci. for the first years, you learn about political theory, local government, etc. you can choose your major in your third year, either polsci major in law and policy studies lps or major in international relations and foreign service irfs. you can enter into the econ minor any time during undergrad, though it's encouraged to take it straight away in the first year so that your course load is not heavy during the third and fourth years.
English_Translation: hello! in usc, all polsci students start out as polsci. for the first years, you learn about political theory, local government, etc. you can choose your major in your third year, either polsci major in law and policy studies lps or major in international relations and foreign service irfs. you can enter the econ minor any time during undergrad, although it is encouraged to take it straight away in the first year so that your course load is not heavy during the third and fourth years.

I got an email saying that I've been accepted to move onto the admissions process so here I am. in the personal data sheet it asks for a student lrn and i have no idea what that is. could anyone also please explain to me about the certificate of eligibility, concept paper template, recommendation form, and personal statement template? thanks for your time!
English_Translation: hi everyone! i'm an international student trying to get enrolled into usc for this fall but i'm a little confused on some things. i got an email saying that i've been accepted to move onto the admissions process so here i am. in the personal data sheet it asks for a student lrn and i have no idea what that is. could anyone also please explain to me about the certificate of eligibility, concept paper template, recommendation form, and personal statement template? thanks for your time!

Data: hello po! the lrn is the learner reference number, a -digit number assigned to students completing the k- program by the department of education in the philippines. I'm not very well-versed in the subject, but I believe the usc admissions application can help you with that at admissions-apply. as for the certificate of eligibility, concept paper template, recommendation form, and the personal statement template; the certificate of eligibility is for currently enrolled shs students who don't have an available school id, the concept paper template is for graduate applicants applying for academic track programs, the recommendation form is for non-usc graduate applicants as they need recommendations to apply for the program, and lastly, the personal statement template is for people applying for the juris doctor program.
English_Translation: hello po! the lrn is the learner reference number, a -digit number assigned to students completing the k- program by the department of education in the philippines. i'm not very well-versed in the subject, but i believe the usc admissions application can help you with that at admissions-apply. as for the certificate of eligibility, concept paper template, recommendation form, and the personal statement template; the certificate of eligibility is for currently enrolled shs students who don't have an available school id, the concept paper template is for graduate applicants applying for academic track programs, the recommendation form is for non-usc graduate applicants as they need recommendations to apply for the program, and lastly, the personal statement template is for people applying for the juris doctor program.

Data: very queer-friendly. the lgbtq community in usc is thriving, just don't exaggerate because the admin is very lenient.
English_Translation: very queer-friendly. the lgbtq community in usc is thriving, just don't exaggerate because the admin is very lenient.

Data: as a queer person myself and a long time carolinian, i can say that although there are still the often offensive comments or jokes that some say regarding the community, it's rather queer friendly here otherwise. being openly queer won't surprise or at least earn you outright disgust even from the most conservative of faculty, but please do note that usc is still a catholic institution. as far as catholic institution goes, usc is rather okay with their treatment of queer students. even some faculty welcome and help students fully embrace their identities while doing their best to balance the rules that usc has. just don't go too overboard and remember to abide by their rules and regulations within the handbook. as long as you don't particularly violate their dress code, you don't have to worry too much about it. oh! by the way, not to alarm you but there is still somewhere in the handbook where they state that "engaging in homosexual relationships activities" is not allowed or is penalized. yeah, you can ignore that. usc really doesn't punish such things unless you make it problematic or scandalous like engaging in "activities" in the restrooms and others similar in inappropriate nature
English_Translation: as a queer person myself and a long time carolinian, i can say that although there are still the often offensive comments or jokes that some say regarding the community, it's rather queer friendly here otherwise. being openly queer won't surprise or at least earn you outright disgust even from the most conservative of faculty, but please do note that usc is still a catholic institution. as far as catholic institution goes, usc is rather okay with their treatment of queer students. even some faculty welcome and help students fully embrace their identities while doing their best to balance the rules that usc has. just don't go too overboard and remember to abide by their rules and regulations within the handbook. as long as you don't particularly violate their dress code, you don't have to worry too much about it. oh! by the way, not to alarm you but there is still somewhere in the handbook where they state that "engaging in homosexual relationships activities" is not allowed or is penalized. yeah, you can ignore that. usc really doesn't punish such things unless you make it problematic or scandalous like engaging in "activities" in the restrooms and others similar in inappropriate nature

Data: im biased for usc as i come from her.. workload is really heavy but mostly depends on professors. the equalizer really are the departmental exams, so if you have a professor who seems to be subpar in discussing topics, you need time supplement it yourself since the departmental exam is uniform.. hit or miss but almost all have either are masters or are lawyers, with few having a phd or professional doctorate degree. young professors are usually lawyers. professors are really diverse so we cannot generalize them. common denominator is that they are really smart. although this does not necessarily translate to being a good teacher.. in recent years, it has become inclusive. i could say it is more inclusive than the other catholic university.. bsabsma community is really great. you will have the opportunity to be exposed to different circles or different interests. the department is really big. students know how to party.

Data: although usc has lined up scholarships coming from external sponsors, there is no guarantee you will be selected. usually, the scholarships are offered to the students garnering the top scores in the entrance examination until the scholarship supply lasts. however, this is not a guarantee as I have a classmate who topped I think the entrance examination yet was not offered for the sole reason that his last name sounded like that. so external scholarships coursed through the usc scholarships office are not entirely merit-based and they consider other factors. the only internal scholarship usc offers is the scholarship they provide to top of the graduating class idk how it is right now as hs graduates are no longer ranked but for one year only. after that, you have to be at the top and with minimum grades of your batch with the same course. for example, to enjoy discount, you have to be top and have a great better than..
English_Translation: although usc has lined up scholarships coming from external sponsors, there is no guarantee you will be selected. usually, the scholarships are offered to the students garnering the top scores in the entrance examination until the scholarship supply lasts. however, this is not a guarantee as i have a classmate who topped the entrance examination yet was not offered for the sole reason that his last name sounded like that. so external scholarships coursed through the usc scholarships office are not entirely merit-based and they consider other factors. the only internal scholarship usc offers is the scholarship they provide to top of the graduating class idk how it is right now as hs graduates are no longer ranked but for one year only. after that, you have to be at the top and with minimum grades of your batch with the same course. for example, to enjoy discount, you have to be top and have a great better than..

Data: I took the entrance exam years ago so not sure if this is still relevant especially the addition of k-program but I hope it helps. entrance exam as far as i can recall test your analytical skills and creative side. part was basic math like elementary level conversion of units, addminusmultiplydivide fraction like basic which i had a hard time since i have been dealing with more complex problem in high school. haha. in part, we were given random shapes and asked to draw something using these shapes, just like random things you can think of. i know it was parts but forgot the other one, maybe essay or iq test. usc came to our school and i did not prepare for the exam. i didn't really think the entrance exam was a big deal, tbh. I passed but almost didn't. I only know one who did not pass and was advised to take landscape architecture. xb; i don't recall them asking for my tor to take the exam since the semester is not yet over. also, anyone who was interested in taking architecture in our school was able to take it and pass. some are not achievers and don't have high grades but they pass so i don't think you have to worry much. xb; you have a difficult journey ahead. lots of sleepless nights, financial burden and self doubt. make sure to find a group of people who can support and help you grow. maximize your college experience by joining organizations and making genuine connections. it's not about the grades you make but the hands you shake at this point. trust me good luck!
English_Translation: i took the entrance exam years ago so not sure if this is still relevant especially the addition of k-program but i hope it helps. entrance exam as far as i can recall test your analytical skills and creative side. part was basic math like elementary level conversion of units, addminusmultiplydivide fraction like basic which i had a hard time since i have been dealing with more complex problem in high school. haha. in part, we were given random shapes and asked to draw something using these shapes, just like random things you can think of. i know it was parts but forgot the other one, maybe essay or iq test. usc came to our school and i did not prepare for the exam. i didn't really think the entrance exam was a big deal, tbh. i passed but almost didn't. i only know one who did not pass and was advised to take landscape architecture. xb; i don't recall them asking for my tor to take the exam since the semester is not yet over. also, anyone who was interested in taking architecture in our school was able to take it and pass. some are not achievers and don't have high grades but they pass so i don't think you have to worry much. xb; you have a difficult journey ahead. lots of sleepless nights, financial burden and self doubt. make sure to find a group of people who can support and help you grow. maximize your college experience by joining organizations and making genuine connections. it's not about the grades you make but the hands you shake at this point. trust me good luck!

Data: hi bb as per my experience dili lalim ang second sem sa first year because daghan mo og units i think if i were to go back wag sana and change the way i did things i would have not taken my minors for granted they would truly keep your gpa afloat and help you if ever you would find your majors difficult and for me the majors were indeed difficult naa na baya dira ang intermediate accounting ninyo cost accounting law on sales governance is okay chill raman na fortify your mind literally ligona jud imong kaugalingon you will find that strength useful along the way too mentally nd sem will be challenging you will be surrounded by peers who seem to understand complicated topics faster than you and though it might be discouraging jud use this to your advantage and gain insights from them too this is important do not be afraid to ask for help i wish i had done that but my self worth really degraded during that semester so dont make the same mistake as i did know its alright to make mistakes because thats the price of learning of course given na sad ang time management here invest and take care of your well being always get enough sleep eat properly and of course if this aligns with what you believe inpray i think so far that is it god bless bb padayon lang

Data: hello from department of accountancy here with the recent announcement of the chairmans list blocks a and b are considered the star sections hence the department will prioritize their blocks schedules in aligning with the prof who has more mastery over a particular subject my advice as someone who tried nonblock during nd year nd sem only go for nonblock if you have other non blockmates with the same nonblock schedule as you or schedule is of the topmost importance due to commute work arrangements especially since youre still first year expect lots of groupworks in the upcoming sem and most students would prioritize their blockmates in their groups hence the risk of you getting thrown into the no choice left group as a nonblock is very very high i enjoyed my nonblock sem because not only did i have friends from the sections i shared schedules with but also because i had fellow nonblockmates who more or less share the same nonblock schedule as i have difference is around subjects and most of the time were the automatic groupmates

Data: pros you wont have to worry about issues that occur in block schedules such as some teachers not being available if you have the schedules beforehand you can plan out your day before enrollment if you choose to you can underload your schedule if need be you can preview the teacher in charge of your class also in case some students recommend other teachers better cons its first come first serve with a lot of students staying up till am just to enroll in their chosen class schedule most of the time the site lags so hard youd spend more time refreshing than picking classes and if youre not quick enough youd be stuck with a class that is either not a favorable time or favorable location you cant have overlap schedules so you have to pick your go to class schedule and have the others adjust to it also theres a chance your chosen class could also get cancelled due to not having enough students in it unlike block classes most of the time we follow the block schedule of our friends for most classes

Data: Hello, if the block sections are not yet full by January, you can opt to enroll during nonblock enrollment following a block schedule. It will be updated soon. So if the block you want is already full, it's gone unless someone swaps with you or the department accommodates you due to valid reasons, such as if you're a working student or an athlete.
English_Translation: hello, if the block sections are not yet full by january, you can opt to enroll during non-block enrollment following a block schedule. it will be updated soon. so if the block you want is already full, it's gone unless someone swaps with you or the department accommodates you due to valid reasons, such as if you're a working student or an athlete.

Data: failing grades in usc is normal feeling sad because of failing grades is normal too especially for first years as an alumni who got plenty of battle scars in the past from usc I can only advise you is just to recover in the second half of the semester and in the finals I hate to say it to you but recovering in the final grade is the only option here just fight against the temptation ps getting passing scores in your exams moving forward is not a guaranteed pass in the final grade ha make sure your passing scores in exams quizzes activities projects etc are big to the point that it won't change your actual failing grade in the midterm grade just fight for the record sauna deepest failing mark I recovered because from when I recovered to when I got to the final grade it also depends on the grading system of the teacher but as far as I can remember after midterm my exams and quizzes usually go between to when I got them so that's what I recovered from the midterm grade into the final grade

Data: might be downvoted for this but at the end of the day i think it might be about respect and hierarchy in the classroom from the teachers perspective he was just asked by his own student for summarized notes its possible he mightve taken offense to that because it looked like a scenario where a boss demands notes from their staff him being the staff im guessing from his generation it was the other way around in the past where classrooms were stricter and less inclusive the students almost never bother the teacher for things it was rarely collaborative basically you get what the teacher gives if theres anything lacking you have to figure it out on your own type of learning so you asking for notes was probably unheard of back in his time im assuming that this is an older professor so that mightve been a boundary drawn from his end im guessing a collaborative learning experience is not what hes used to also the cash grabbing from his students isnt right i agree i guess my point is to also try and empathize where your professor is coming from im not defending your professor but just wanted to give my two cents on the situation

Data: yes its definitely doable a lot of my classmates worked fulltime or parttime while in law school and finished it takes discipline of course but its absolutely possible and honestly very impressive to see people do both tuition depends on your load but for a full load you can expect around k per sem books can be pricey usually k per book but to be honest you can thrive using ebooks transcripts case digests and the library i bought codals during st year but barely used them never bought na nd year onward depends though some like writing on their codals i like printing it out cause its bigger i guess just figure out what works best for you teaching style varies per prof but in general expect recits some are theoryheavy some are casefocused socratic in general if you fail subjects you can retake just remember theres a unit cutoff if you go beyond that youll need to transfer no formal grading curve in usc law it really depends on the prof some are generous some are very stringy in grading

Data: hello graduating shs student here ill answer your question based on the categories youve given academics if you want a relatively chill shs experience dont come here its really not for the weak first it may seem like its any other shs however scsc loves events its unavoidable youll flip out at some point if you choose here its just part of the experience research is a very big deal here research create a product out of said research and overall it sometimes feel like youre already doing your bachelors study i cant say for every research will feel like that it highly depends on your topic although a nice feature in the system is that you do not strictly have to align it to your strand like im a humss student but i could participate in a stem research in the final year g youre allowed to mix in terms of members it doesnt have to strictly be the same strand as you that you have to work with however in res your grades are heavily dependent on yourself and your group theyre not going to baby you here your res advisor is not built to remind you and etc they will guide you but dont expect your advisor to spoon feed you anymore student treatment administration its not heaven it really isnt theres a reason why a certain school here in cebu and scsc are being joked that theyre in a competition of whos more ct and while im not saying that theyre all bad its justlets not student treatment teaching faculty if you wanted a more advanced curriculum they definitely have that compared to most schools luckily its not scihigh type of advanced more of like your bases are definitely covered for sure like any other school there are good ones and bad ones bad at teaching bad in terms of personal boundaries or just a terror teacher in general the quality of your teachers is kind of like gambling although most teachers are definitely qualified because theyre either doctorates or have done or is currently doing their masters degree student organizationsclubs its actually a gem here you actually get meaningful experiences and learn as its currently proactive because its students who really run the orgs clubs meaning it genuinely acts in your interest most especially if you applied for a club to learn gain experience or if you want that good addition to your resume you get practical applications of your interests here its not just a simple club where Data: freshie here sa uscscsc the school is very good and i believe the curriculum is difficult but compared to other schools its not so bad school tasks are not so hard as well ang campus namo kay north campus and not south anymore exams for all strands this semester kay ai generated tapos multiple choice halos tanan except for genmath what strand r u planning to pursue for humss most of teachers are ok theyre very good at teaching stem is the one harder to get into because of the grades cutoff plus daghan kaau mag stem na karon daghan kaaug blocks ang stem here ang hell week namo kay pt week since the teachers ask for a lot for the projects exams werent that hard this semester keep in mind that this is a very big school so teachers teach multiple blocks to cater to all students and they may not be able reply to your emailmessages at all because they get a hundred or more emails per day deadlines are also strict and dili mu consider jud ang teacher unless valid ang reason this was a culture shock for me coming from a smaller school dili ni sila mu apas ug students here in fact they dont really adjust grades if hopeless ang student they have to pay more for summer school if i had to say right now i dont regret coming here my classmates are very competitive with their studies and it helps me grow pud with that type of environment my old school was hopeless so definitely culture shock kaau
English_Translation: freshie here at uscscsc the school is very good and i believe the curriculum is difficult but compared to other schools its not so bad school tasks are not so hard as well our campus is north campus and not south anymore exams for all strands this semester kay ai generated tapos multiple choice almost everything except for genmath what strand r u planning to pursue for humss most of teachers are ok theyre very good at teaching stem is the one harder to get into because of the grades cutoff plus there are a lot of stem now and many blocks stem here is our hell week because pt week since the teachers ask for a lot for the projects exams werent that hard this semester keep in mind that this is a very big school so teachers teach multiple blocks to cater to all students and they may not be able to reply to your emailmessages at all because they get a hundred or more emails per day deadlines are also strict and you don't consider the teacher unless the reason is valid this was a culture shock for me coming from a smaller school they don't catch up and students here in fact they dont really adjust grades if the student is hopeless they have to pay more for summer school if i had to say right now i dont regret coming here my classmates are very competitive with their studies and it helps me grow also with that type of environment my old school was hopeless so definitely culture shock kaau

Data: hi graduated in scsc uscshs back in my day i was a stem student and i wouldnt say the curriculum is bad but i think its not as demanding as other senior high schools like science high siguro research ofc is always tiring and theres gonna be deadweights no matter where you go and its also senior high school a step up jhs and a step below college its gonna be harder than jhs no matter what i enjoyed the curriculum as there were days we spent mostly in the lab for science subjects not sure if its the same now and the teachers may be demanding but theres only a few teachers who werent easy to approach i slept a lot during class and the teachers let me be the classrooms are airconditioned and the facilities in north campus arent the best particularly the crs some toilets dont flush and some days theres no water flowing from the faucets but afaik the facilities were being upgraded in my time so im not sure on that i mainly chose usc shs in preparation for college in usc so getting a taste of the usc culture before going to college helped me transition into college life a bit easier

Data: first week of classes because you will have orientation plus discussion on intro to law short course so that's your first exam most of the time the professors really send the syllabus days before the class starts download canvas because you can add them there and they will send the syllabus modules and other reference materials also on canvas so post esp because you don't have a block rep hopefully you have a block group chat or have been added to the block gc so that you will know any updates right away if you get timings from professors who are still reciting on the first day you should recite on the first day however most professors because they are still doing introductions and when the next meeting is the reciting you should probably just have something when you enter the class like the background of the subject or whatnot buy codals if you can because you will finish it hehe codals don't buy commentaries because it depends on you professor what book they recommend you can ask them when you first meet USC doesn't have a strict dress code but try to wear something appropriate too because judges, prosecutors, attorneys, and your opponents, if you vlog, just make sure you don't disturb the classes or the library. There is a small store on the third floor of Kutch so if you're hungry, you can buy ramen there. There is also a wrockledge downstairs. Please note that the Pelaez gate is closed because of the renovation of the entrance, so you can go there through the Sanciangko gate near the USC gym or through the Schotel gate. That's all I can think of for now. If you have any concerns or questions, I'll be happy to address them. Good luck.
English_Translation: first week of classes because you will have orientation plus discussion on intro to law short course so that's your first exam most of the time the professors really send the syllabus days before the class starts download canvas because you can add them there and they will send the syllabus modules and other reference materials also on canvas so post esp because you don't have a block rep hopefully you have a block group chat or have been added to the block gc so that you will know any updates right away if you get timings from professors who are still reciting on the first day you should recite on the first day however most professors because they are still doing introductions and when the next meeting is the reciting you should probably just have something when you enter the class like the background of the subject or whatnot buy codals if you can because you will finish it hehe codals don't buy commentaries because it depends on you professor what book they recommend you can ask them when you first meet usc doesn't have a strict dress code but try to wear something appropriate too because judges, prosecutors, attorneys, and your opponents, if you vlog, just make sure you don't disturb the classes or the library. there is a small store on the third floor of kutch so if you're hungry, you can buy ramen there. there is also a wrockledge downstairs. please note that the pelaez gate is closed because of the renovation of the entrance, so you can go there through the sanciangko gate near the usc gym or through the schotel gate. that's all i can think of for now. if you have any concerns or questions, i'll be happy to address them. good luck.

Data: hi anon i assure you youll be fine as long as you remain determined yes the workload is heavy but youll get used to it you just have to take note of the things that work best for you i am certainly not the most organized person and i am admittedly a chronic crammer but what gets me through difficult exam seasons is that i know what works best for me study techniques notetaking systems etc at the end of the day its all about knowing how to lift your own weight in spite of the given circumstances as for the other necessary skills is you have to be good at public speaking during my first year of uni we had presentations and recits multiple times a week then by the time we got to sophomore year all of this was amped up into a daily routine lol we had debates presentations and recits on rotation every other day the most crucial thing here is that you have to always always be confident and prepared sheer confidence is good but confidence that is rooted in preparedness will never fail you also the profs there are top tierrr no bs some are strict and even regarded as terror but in the long run youll be grateful for them ive become more detailoriented and meticulous thanks to the terror profs ive been under as for the mun dont worry about it yet your profs will orient you about it months before the competition just make sure to read the guidelines because it was honestly confusing as well for me and the others and yes it is similar to other muns because its a simulation of them so its essentially done to mimic the real deal lastly the tqa is taken online its more of an assessment than a test technically you can choose between the two lps or irfs if you qualify for both majors based on your tqa results however if you qualify in only major then i think thats your sole bet although im not so sure about how rigid this rule is ba jud as someone who qualified for both majors dont worry too much about this yet though because the department will orient you on this naman in due time thats all hope this helps good luck

Data: Although the professors are well-known psychologists in Cebu, most of them teach at USC, Sir Glenn, our dean is the president of the Psychological Association of the Philippines, though I don't know if the same professors are still in the undergrad program. Our professors in the undergrad program are our idols in their wisdom and teaching. They should resign. But currently, our professors in the masters program are good. You will appreciate their teaching more because they were equipped when we were undergrad. My classmates who are not at USC are undergrads. They will make a little extra effort to catch up on what they missed out on. They are high standards and sometimes difficult. But expect that. If you really want good quality of education, I agree with the lack of exposure. I would have benefited more if I had tried a clinical setting for my internship. They focus more on the industrial setting. Guidance is a must because only a minority of graduates will proceed to become psychologists. Yes, there are other professors who are inconsistent and it's a shame because there are so many of them who could impart knowledge. You are a classy person who is bright but not good at teaching hahaha fortunately there are few of them in the faculty, it's just that the psych department and their instructors are topnotchers even on the board

Data: hello id like to clarify something since i have reread the student manual to graduate with honors a student must carry the required load per term continuously and without interruption as prescribed in the curriculum pursued unless the student obtains an approved leave of absence for a maximum of one academic year as stipulated in section have completed in usc at least of the total number of academic units and have been in residence for at least three consecutive years immediately prior to graduation carry a minimum study load of units and a workload of at least six hours per day if heshe is a working student for gainful employment provided that heshe can submit on the date of application for graduation these pieces of evidence of hisher gainful employment a certification of employment by hisher employer and b sss membership documents or any other pertinent document acceptable to the awards and graduation committee agc possess good moral character and not have incurred a failing grade including nstp and physical education tpe courses transferees with unsatisfactory grades in the previous school may be considered for latin honors provided that heshe is enrolled in a different program in usc however shiftees with unsatisfactory grades in the previous programs are disqualified from graduating with honors a student may qualify for honors even if heshe has been underloaded for units in a semester for a maximum of two semesters the underload may also be justified if heshe is a working scholar or a varsity player or a member of the performing arts group of the university usc student manual
English_Translation: hello id like to clarify something since i have reread the student manual to graduate with honors a student must carry the required load per term continuously and without interruption as prescribed in the curriculum pursued unless the student obtains an approved leave of absence for a maximum of one academic year as stipulated in section have completed in usc at least of the total number of academic units and have been in residence for at least three consecutive years immediately prior to graduation carry a minimum study load of units and a workload of at least six hours per day if heshe is a working student for gainful employment provided that heshe can submit on the date of application for graduation these pieces of evidence of hisher gainful employment a certification of employment by hisher employer and b sss membership documents or any other pertinent document acceptable to the awards and graduation committee agc possess good moral character and have not incurred a failing grade including nstp and physical education tpe courses transferees with unsatisfactory grades in the previous school may be considered for latin honors provided that heshe is enrolled in a different program in usc however shiftees with unsatisfactory grades in the previous programs are disqualified from graduating with honors a student may qualify for honors even if heshe has been underloaded for units in a semester for a maximum of two semesters the underload may also be justified if heshe is a working scholar or a varsity player or a member of the performing arts group of the university usc student manual

Data: curriculum im not entirely familiar with it but i hear its good its rare to see a uni here in cebu offer marine biology as a program you can probably ask the bio department for their prospectus list of subjects for the entire curriculum if youre interested in pursuing further studies such as a masters etc its definitely possible especially if you perform well academically as for the subjects there will be a lot of subjects you have to take that are not really related to your program but every college student in the philippines has to go through like communication arts pe etc it often gets hectic during the semester because of the busy schedule and workload especially since usc tends to put a lot of subjects per semester by the th year you will accomplish your undergraduate thesis usually with a group of other people early morning classes can also go as early as am usc is a catholic school as well so there will be required subjects on religion and philosophy you can join orgs since there is a lot and you can make friends there as well extracurriculars can make your campus life quite fun campus its the biggest campus in cebu and is really quite nice there is a big library stadium etc and its on a mountainside so a lot of nature and a lot of hiking it can get quite hot especially in the uniform however as the other comments said there will definitely be traffic so plan where you stay accordingly there are many places to eat both inside and outside and the jeepneys that pass go to some malls where you can go to buy stuff
English_Translation: curriculum im not entirely familiar with it but i hear its good its rare to see a uni here in cebu offer marine biology as a program you can probably ask the bio department for their prospectus list of subjects for the entire curriculum if youre interested in pursuing further studies such as a masters etc its definitely possible especially if you perform well academically as for the subjects there will be a lot of subjects you have to take that are not really related to your program but every college student in the philippines has to go through like communication arts pe etc it often gets hectic during the semester because of the busy schedule and workload especially since usc tends to put a lot of subjects per semester by the th year you will accomplish your undergraduate thesis usually with a group of other people early morning classes can also go as early as am usc is a catholic school as well so there will be required subjects on religion and philosophy you can join orgs since there is a lot and you can make friends there as well extracurriculars can make your campus life quite fun campus its the biggest campus in cebu and is really quite nice there is a big library stadium etc and its on a mountainside so a lot of nature and a lot of hiking it can get quite hot especially in the uniform however as the other comments said there will definitely be traffic so plan where you stay accordingly there are many places to eat both inside and outside and the jeepneys that pass go to some malls where you can go to buy stuff

Data: hi anon not a mbio student but heres some things i know talking with some colleagues from marine bio a lot of the courses are practical work especially after you guys receive your certification in diving they would spend a lot of time in the marine bio station for their major subjects like even a whole day cause they need to check samples in a large interval iirc in the near future one of their practical exams require them to be able to read underwater it sounds really physically demanding from someone whose course is mostly just theoretical lol but definitely its worth it if it floats your boat workload might be slightly less demanding since youre an international student youll be exempted from nstp national service training program generally about service and disaster preparedness where classes would be ever saturday for the first year also therell be a lot of minor classes too im not sure if youll be exempted from lwr life works of rizal and rph readings in philippine history both are fun classes about the history of our national hero and history but its quite demanding too cause of the amount of readings expect too that in minor subjects instead of a final exam youll have a group project have that projects in subjects at once and it gets chaotic honestly for the dormitory theres a school dorm but its not really the best bathrooms are communal and the food in the canteen is decent you dont have to pay for electricity though so unlimited airconditioning promise that aircon will save you cause of how hot in the philippines get but also wifi access is quite limited cause school wifi restricts some apps even restricts netflix however it might be a good place to start out since its safe and commute would not be a hassle ill send this thread to a friend from mbio so that theres someone who can fill you in all the deets

Data: hello i had the same set up took up stem and then proceeded to polsci and currently in law school for me it really took a whole sem to adjust in polsci after being used to face with numbers i had to learn how to read tons of readings and induced myself to appreciate the readings and read faster too bc in polsci the pacing is fast the tons of reading you will have today might not be the topic for the next meeting so u really have to catch up fast one thing i can say is that tho you rlly have to love what ure doing learning and loving polsci came too easy for me because i know was meant for it ive also excelled academically because of it so u have to love it because polsci is not for the ones who dont read tbh lolol but also u have to have proper study habits so as early as now practice that time management and study wise as well you should expect oral exercises and recits if not everyday then more often than often hahahah and ur exams are usually essays teachers are great also they are knowledgeable with what they teach but they get rawr during thesis but its ok u just have to prove that ure competent as for the readings start with the basics i e philippine government general readings and the works of aristotle on politics and government as the latter will largely determine if u wanna stay in polsci ky mostly ing ana gyud ang readings ipanghatag hahaha medyo boring gyud siya na read hahaha but mao jd ni foundation nato hope this helps
English_Translation: hello i had the same set up took up stem and then proceeded to polsci and currently in law school for me it really took a whole sem to adjust in polsci after being used to face with numbers i had to learn how to read tons of readings and induced myself to appreciate the readings and read faster too bc in polsci the pacing is fast the tons of reading you will have today might not be the topic for the next meeting so u really have to catch up fast one thing i can say is that tho you rlly have to love what ure doing learning and loving polsci came too easy for me because i know was meant for it ive also excelled academically because of it so u have to love it because polsci is not for the ones who dont read tbh lolol but also u have to have proper study habits so as early as now practice that time management and study wise as well you should expect oral exercises and recites if not everyday then more often than often hahahah and ur exams are usually essays teachers are great also they are knowledgeable with what they teach but they get rawr during thesis but its ok u just have to prove that ure competent as for the readings start with the basics i e philippine government general readings and the works of aristotle on politics and government as the latter will largely determine if u wanna stay in polsci ky mostly that's what the readings will be given hahaha it's really boring to read hahaha but that's our foundation hope this helps

Data: the uniform is still the same some incoming st year students opt to buy or borrow from current or former pharma students to have the unif patterns be copied by a seamstress of their choice color pattern and fabric type make sure to really make it identical the chair and dean are observant yes it is required to be worn daily shcp has that rule even for halfday sat classes nd year and above the usuals for lab would be long sleeved lab coat latex gloves masks hair net and eye goggles in addition you will have to buy locks for your lab cabinets dishwashing soap sponge test tube brush circular rags white mantle to cover your table area and alcohol microbio lab nd year you only need to worry about the items below as you reach certain semesters just make sure to have the basics for st year nd sem to further years large amber bottles and small amber bottles wide mouth screw capped for nd year st sem you will need so much amber bottles cream containers and few squeeze bottles make sure to retain some of your bottles and containers since it would be useful in the future lab activities or for your thesis
English_Translation: the uniform is still the same some incoming st year students opt to buy or borrow from current or former pharma students to have the unif patterns be copied by a seamstress of their choice color pattern and fabric type make sure to really make it identical the chair and dean are observant yes it is required to be worn daily shcp has that rule even for half day sat classes nd year and above the usuals for lab would be long sleeved lab coat latex gloves masks hair net and eye goggles in addition you will have to buy locks for your lab cabinets dishwashing soap sponge test tube brush circular rags white mantle to cover your table area and alcohol microbio lab nd year you only need to worry about the items below as you reach certain semesters just make sure to have the basics for st year nd sem to further years large amber bottles and small amber bottles wide mouth screw capped for nd year st sem you will need so many amber bottles cream containers and few squeeze bottles make sure to retain some of your bottles and containers since it would be useful in the future lab activities or for your thesis

Data: hi someone else had the same concern as you on here and this was my reply in case it helps you sad hi i cant give recommendations regarding usjr bsa being better but i can say that your initial course placement in usc does not matter the only actual aspect would be a sort of separation from your peers where bsmabsia may be looked down on but from my experience this is very rare and it does not matter at all since all major subjects for the first years of all accounting courses in usc are the same and all students under accounting courses get blocked together after the st sem you can shift after st sem but it isnt suggested as there is no point to it the shifting of courses usually happen during part after your second year to qualify for bsa you must have all major subjects above cut off you may take repeat classes if you dont pass cutoff and pass part dont feel too bad about not initially qualifying for bsa as there are so many factors in actually passing the course and the entrance exam is not really one of those factors the department just feels like separating the students hope this helps

Data: hi took the cae last april for bsa and thankfully passed i honestly didnt review much for the exam aside for the college readiness test which in our case is abm based for someone na way background sa abm as tvl graduate ko i studied basic concepts ra for the first part of the exam iq test its made up of abstract verbal and numerical reasoning abstract is items verbal is items and numerical is items pud i cant recall how much time is dedicated for each subtest pero overall for the cae is minutes the college readiness test crt is made up of items for abm di ko sure if pila sa uban nga strandbased exams with minutes to answer everything being completely honest hours ra ako review time since i booked my schedule last minute the day before for the questions naa jud uban na wa koy idea about and gijema ra its mostly a matter of analyzing and common sense ayaw siya eovercomplicate kay dali ra makaoverwhelm i advise you to do practice questions on basic accountancy or abm analyze each question you can even use ai to explain things na medjo complicated para mafamiliar sa possible questions same goes for the iq test charge up your mind before the exam wake up early and do some meditation to relax maybe answer a few puzzles and pray if religious ka haha overall analysis and timemanagement imo bff para sa cae anyway relax ra and best of luck
English_Translation: hi took the cae last april for bsa and thankfully passed i honestly didnt review much for the exam aside for the college readiness test which in our case is abm based for someone who has no background in abm as tvl graduate i only studied basic concepts for the first part of the exam iq test its made up of abstract verbal and numerical reasoning abstract is items verbal is items and numerical is items also i cant recall how much time is dedicated for each subtest but overall for the cae is minutes the college readiness test crt is made up of items for abm i'm not sure how many of the other strandbased exams with minutes to answer everything being completely honest i only have hours to review since i booked my schedule last minute the day before for the questions there are others i have no idea about and gijema ra it's mostly a matter of analyzing and common sense don't overcomplicate it because it's easy to overwhelm i advise you to do practice questions on basic accountancy or abm analyze each question you can even use ai to explain things that are quite complicated be familiar with possible questions same goes for the iq test charge up your mind before the exam wake up early and do some meditation to relax maybe answer a few puzzles and pray if you are religious haha ​​overall analysis and time management your bff for cae anyway just relax and best of luck
